select_when: A visible metric shift, pattern, or association is being treated as proof of what is driving the outcome.
The team is about to act on a symptom because the presenting variable is measurable, even though the causal mechanism is still uncertain.
Interventions are expensive or hard to reverse, so acting on the wrong driver would waste time, budget, or trust.
Diagnosis needs to separate proximate causes from root causes instead of confusing the latest event with the true generative mechanism.
avoid_when: The evidence base is observational, confounded, or incomplete, but the recommendation is still being framed as if causality were already settled.
The work only needs a quick associative signal for exploration, but the team is pretending that full causal proof is available before learning starts.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on mechanism tracing, confound control, and root-cause discipline rather than on broad statistics survey material.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor-shaped analytics sprawl because the raw source is strongest on mechanism, causal chain rigor, and root-cause tracing.
Did not add higher-order composition fields because this batch remains Wave 1 to Wave 3 only.
open_questions: The second avoid-when item is conservative reviewed normalization over a source that is stronger on anti-false-certainty than on explicit exploratory activation boundaries.

select_when.note: The raw source is explicit that the model is strongest when visible associations are mistaken for drivers and root causes still need to be established.

avoid_when.note: The causal-certainty warning is explicit in the source; the exploration-oriented anti-activation condition is reviewed normalization.

input_type.note: The raw model activates on mechanism and driver questions, but the compact input label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The source produces tested causal pathways and root-cause framing; the output label compresses that into a typed artifact.

reasoning_types.note: The source is strongly causal and diagnostic, but the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
